mainmatter.p: To define the bottom element of A, we choose \alpha { \mathopen {} \left ( \bot , \lambda \_ . \star \right ) \mathclose {}}. Fixing n \in A, we must check that \alpha { \mathopen {} \left ( \bot , \lambda \_ . \star \right ) \mathclose {}} \sqsubseteq n. Because \mathsf { L } is a Kock–Zöberlein monad and thus we have \alpha \dashv \eta _{A}, it is equivalent to observe that { \mathopen {} \left ( \bot , \lambda \_ . \star \right ) \mathclose {}} \sqsubseteq \eta _{A}n.
Let { \mathopen {} \left ( A, \alpha \right ) \mathclose {}} be a \mathbb {L}-algebra; then the underlying dcpo A is pointed.
